commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a bounded local loopback `status` RPC fallback for CLI status/gateway-status/daemon probe paths, guarded `callGateway` loopback overrides, regression tests, and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked reports describe a local loopback probe timeout or unknown capability while direct `status`/`--require-rpc` succeeds, and current main still returns raw probe failures in daemon and gateway-status paths.
commands.review_followup: Automerge should stay paused until the exact-head core-support-boundary/checks-node-core failure is investigated or repaired; the available metadata does not identify a safe file-level automated repair.

commands.review_summary:
This PR replaces the Anthropic Vertex SDK with a local `BaseAnthropic`/Google Auth client, updates package and lockfile dependencies, adds header-preservation coverage, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. The exact-head GitHub check annotations reproduce both blockers: the Anthropic Vertex manifest misses `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, and root `google-auth-library` violates the Google Chat plugin-local dependency contract.
commands.review_followup: The PR is automerge-opted but currently has merge conflicts plus dependency-contract failures; a maintainer should rebase/resolve and decide the dependency-ownership shape before automerge continues.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Feishu DM sender-name resolution through `chatMembers` before contact fallback, account-scoped sender-name caches, permission-guidance propagation, topic-label tests, and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. A focused unit test can mock `chatMembers.get` to return a single member whose `member_id` differs from the inbound `senderId`; the PR code currently returns that non-matching name instead of falling through safely.
commands.review_followup: Human review is required because this protected automerge PR is dirty/unmergeable and has security-sensitive P2 findings, so the next action is maintainer/security follow-up rather than a normal repair queue.
